title: Omphalandria oppositifolia
intro:
I couldn't find any information on Omphalandria oppositifolia. However, I found information on Omphalodes and Omphalodes oppositifolia. Here's a paragraph on Omphalodes oppositifolia:

Omphalodes oppositifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the kidney-leafed bugloss. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Omphalodes oppositifolia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Omphalodes oppositifolia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
benefits: Omphalandria oppositifolia, also known as the opposite-leaved honeysuckle, is a plant with various uses in traditional medicine. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever and pain. It is also thought to have antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and infections. The plant's leaves and flowers are often used to make teas and infusions, which are said to have a calming effect on the mind and body. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Omphalandria oppositifolia's health benefits.
side_effects: Omphalandria oppositifolia is a plant species, but I couldn't find any reliable information about its side effects. However, if you are referring to the Omphalanthus or Omphalodes species (also known as navelworts), they are generally considered non-toxic. Some sources report that Omphalanthus species may cause mild gastrointestinal irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. In rare cases, ingestion of these plants may lead to more severe symptoms, but further research is needed to confirm this. As with any plant, it's essential to consult with a medical professional or a qualified botanist for accurate information on its potential effects.
